title: Ochthochloa compressa
intro: Ochthochloa compressa, also known as Bristle Bent or Bristle-grass, is a perennial grass species native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Ochthochloa compressa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Ochthochloa compressa is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, as well as in the production of tinctures and extracts, which are often used to support respiratory health and immune function.
